title: Sour Crumbly Argentinian Green Beans Salad
description: A bright, low‑carb Argentinian salad with blanched green beans, crumbled queso fresco, lemon‑vinegar dressing, and a splash of festive flavor.
instructions: Heat a medium pot of salted water to a boil.
Add the green beans and blanch for 3 minutes, until bright green and just tender.
Immediately transfer the beans to an ice bath to stop the cooking process; drain and set aside to cool fully.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, red wine vinegar, lemon juice, minced garlic, salt, and pepper to make the dressing.
In a large bowl, combine the cooled beans, diced bell pepper, cherry tomato halves, crumbled queso fresco, and chopped parsley.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss gently to coat everything evenly.
If using, sprinkle the capers over the top for an extra burst of briny flavor.
Serve the salad chilled or at room temperature for the freshest taste.
